title: Climate scientists aren't surprised by summer's record heat: 'We've entered a dangerous zone'
subhed: Record heat, historic drought and sweeping wildfires have marked France's 2026 summer. Yet climate scientists mostly see global warming's expected effects playing out. Only the rapid succession of heatwaves has caught them off guard.

title: China's AI doctrine is a challenge for Europe

Iran bets on war of attrition against the US
subhed: As US airstrikes persist in Iran, Iranian authorities are, for now, avoiding a full-scale confrontation. Their strategy is to leverage the threat of global energy disruption to force Washington to reconsider its approach.

title: Two months before Russian parliamentary elections, antiwar voices still struggle for traction
subhed: The Kremlin continues to stifle the few parliamentary candidates opposed to the war in Ukraine, even as the public and some industrialists begin to imagine peace.

title: Between China and the US, a new diplomacy of detainees emerges
subhed: Alongside the major issues discussed between the world's two leading powers, the fate of American citizens imprisoned in China on espionage charges has also become a bargaining chip.

title: Bernard Arnault, a 'free-marketeer' who whispers in the ear of every president
subhed: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (2/6). LVMH's boss has never hidden his allegiance to France's pro-business, establishment right. He's close to fellow billionaire Vincent Bolloré and, above all, pragmatic. Now he's preparing for the far right's rise to power.

In Paris café, terror attack survivors find community: 'People want to tell each other: I am alive'
subhed: After long days attending the 2015 Paris attacks trial, survivors and relatives of victims met in a bar to comfort one another. They have continued to gather every month, joined by victims of other attacks, to share experiences, advice and, above all, laughter and good company.

title: Didier Deschamps bids farewell to French team after spectacular World Cup defeat
subhed: The coach stepped down after a dramatic 6-4 loss in the World Cup third-place match against England on Saturday. Over his 14 years in the role, he nonetheless managed to restore France to the pinnacle of world football.

title: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
subhed: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.
